add-multiple-records.label: Add multiple records
add-multiple-records.summary: Learn how to insert multiple rows into a table in a single SQL statement, and understand best practices for adding data safely.

q1.title: Insert two new inventory items in one statement

q2.title: Insert multiple rows with NULL notes

q3.title: Insert rows omitting columns with defaults

q1.title: Insert two new inventory items in one statement
q1.prompt:
Write a SQL statement to add these two items to the `inventory_items` table:

- Name: 'Mini Notebook', Category: 'Accessories', Price: 5.00, Status: 'active', Is_test: 0, Last_updated: '2026-02-12'
- Name: 'Wall Calendar', Category: 'Decor', Price: 14.00, Status: 'draft', Is_test: 0, Last_updated: '2026-02-12'

Use an explicit column list and insert both rows in a single statement.
q1.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q2.title: Insert multiple rows with NULL notes
q2.prompt:
Add two new items to `inventory_items` in a single statement. Both should have `notes` as NULL:

- Name: 'Travel Mug', Category: 'Kitchen', Price: 12.50, Status: 'active', Is_test: 0, Last_updated: '2026-02-13'
- Name: 'Art Print', Category: 'Decor', Price: 30.00, Status: 'active', Is_test: 0, Last_updated: '2026-02-13'

Include the `notes` column and set it to NULL for both rows.
q2.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q3.title: Insert rows omitting columns with defaults
q3.prompt:
Insert two new items into `inventory_items` in a single statement. Omit the `status` and `is_test` columns so their default values are used:

- Name: 'Sticker Sheet', Category: 'Accessories', Price: 3.00, Last_updated: '2026-02-14'
- Name: 'Ceramic Vase', Category: 'Decor', Price: 28.00, Last_updated: '2026-02-14'

Do not include `status` or `is_test` in your column list.
q3.hint: Focus on the SQL task being asked for, not on copying final query text.

help.concept: Build the query from the operation the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which clauses or functions are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the query based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q4.title: Benefits and requirements of multi-row INSERT
q4.prompt: Which of the following statements about inserting multiple rows in one SQL statement are true? Select all that apply.
q4.hint: Think about efficiency and syntax requirements.

help.concept: Multi-row INSERT statements improve efficiency and require each value set to match the column list.
help.hint_1: Consider how multi-row INSERT affects performance and what must be true about the values.
help.hint_2: Remember that all value sets must have the same number and order of values as the columns listed.

options.a: a. It is more efficient to insert multiple rows in one statement than to use separate statements.
options.b: b. All sets of values must match the column list in number and order.
options.c: c. You can use different columns for each row in the same statement.
options.d: d. Multi-row INSERT is only possible if all columns are NOT NULL.
options.e: e. You can insert rows with NULL values if the column allows it.

q5.title: Syntax for multiple row insertion
q5.prompt: Choose the best value for the first missing blank in the statement.
q5.hint: Think about how you separate items in a list.

help.concept: In SQL, multiple sets of values in an INSERT statement are separated by a specific punctuation mark.
help.hint_1: It's the same symbol used to separate items in a list.
help.hint_2: Look at the example: ('a', 1), ('b', 2). What separates the two sets?

sk1.title: Inserting Multiple Rows at Once
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
When you want to add several new records to a table, you can use a single `INSERT INTO` statement with multiple sets of values. This is more efficient than running separate insert statements for each row.

For example, to add two new inventory items:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES
  ('Enamel Pin', 'Accessories', 3.50, 'active', 0, '2026-02-10'),
  ('Desk Mat', 'Decor', 22.00, 'draft', 0, '2026-02-10');
```

Each set of values in parentheses represents a new row. All sets must match the column list in order and type.

sk2.title: Why Use Explicit Column Lists?
sk2.bodyMarkdown:
It's best practice to specify the column names in your `INSERT INTO` statement. This makes your code safer and clearer, especially if the table structure changes later. If you omit the column list, you must provide values for every column in the table, in the exact order they appear in the schema.

Example with explicit columns:

```sql
INSERT INTO inventory_items (name, category, price, status, is_test, last_updated)
VALUES ('Keychain', 'Accessories', 2.99, 'active', 0, '2026-02-11');
```

This approach avoids errors if new columns are added to the table in the future.
